---
title: "Setup & Prerequisites"
toc: true
---

Complete this before the workshop. Allow about 30–45 minutes, plus download time for the
spatial data.

## 1. Software

- **R** ≥ 4.3: <https://cran.rstudio.com/>
- **RStudio** (latest): <https://posit.co/downloads/>
- Hardware: 8 GB RAM or more (16 GB preferred), stable internet.

## 2. R packages

Run the bundled install script once:

```r
source("resources/install_required_packages.R")
```

It installs: `tidyverse`, `here`, `patchwork`, `janitor`, `rio`, `sf`, `ggspatial`,
`raster`, `exactextractr`, `tidygeocoder`, `leaflet`, `mapview`, `spdep`, `rgeoda`,
`HistData`.

::: {.callout-warning}
## System dependencies for `sf`
`sf`, `rgeoda` and `exactextractr` need GDAL, GEOS and PROJ. On macOS:
`brew install gdal geos proj`. On Ubuntu: `apt install libgdal-dev libgeos-dev libproj-dev`.
On Windows the CRAN binaries include these, so no extra steps.
:::

## 3. Project & data

1. Open `Module2_SpatialEpi.Rproj` in RStudio. This sets the working directory so every
   `here()` path resolves.
2. The spatial data is in `spatial_files/` and `data/` (already in the repo). If you cloned
   without the large files, re-extract `spatial_files.zip` into the project root.

## 4. Pre-flight check

```r
library(sf); library(tidyverse); library(here)
# prints the number of India state polygons:
sf::st_read(here("spatial_files", "India_State_Boundary.shp")) |> nrow()
```

A non-zero row count means you are ready. See [GIS-basics.pdf](resources/GIS-basics.pdf) for a
primer, and `resources/prerequisites.qmd` for more detail.
